title: Sikorsky H-34 Choctaw: Vietnam War’s Unsung Hero?
description: In a world liberally populated with Hueys, Cobras, Loaches and Chinooks, the piston-driven H-34 was lyrically obsolete. However, it was the only thing available piloted by the only handy lunatic willing to lay it all on the line
author: The Gun People

title: Deadly Decisions: Do You Make These Mistakes?
description: You may not realize it, but criminals interview potential victims. Often, a predator will do a quick analysis to see if you look like a good target.
author: The Gun People

title: M21 Sniper Rifle — A Short History
description: In Vietnam, the U.S. military quickly recognized the need for accurate long-range fire to engage distant targets and conduct counter-sniper operations. Early in the Vietnam conflict, marksmen relied on aging M1C and D Garands from World War II. Some fortunate snipers received accurized M14 rifles used in marksmanship competitions.
author: The Gun People

title: 7 Best Turbo Charged Weapon Lights in 2026 (Our...
description: It’s so bright we gotta wear shades around today’s weapon lights. The modern era of weapon-mounted lights has gotten brighter, smaller, lighter, and offer more range, more power, and ultimately a more capable tool. Lights are evolving and evolving quickly; with that in mind, we wanted to bring you the best high-powered weapon-mounted lights.
author: The Gun People

title: My Unpopular Opinion: Don’t Customize Your Gun
description: Many times, the customization and addition of accessories will run you more than the upfront cost of the gun. I’ll usually add an optic because of its obvious benefits. But let’s say you wind up also replacing the furniture and sights as well with it, and you can quickly find yourself with a very high price tag
author: The Gun People

title: Dominate the Dark: Low Light Primer for the Armed Citizen
description: Just about all of us are a little apprehensive when light falls to low levels and we can’t see what is going on around us. It’s not the darkness, but what the darkness may be hiding.
author: The Gun People

title: Blue Dragons — Korean Marines in the Vietnam War
description: The area was swarming with stocky guys in Duck Hunter camouflage. A guidon flapping in a wet sea breeze indicated the compound contained elements of the Blue Dragons. We were in amongst a battalion of the 2nd Korean Marine Brigade scheduled to shortly kick off an operations titled Dragon Fire
author: The Gun People

title: How to Mount a Rifle Scope — Tips & Tricks You Need to Know
description: From my Diamondback Tactical scope to my Springfield Armory SAINT Victor rifle... to mount a rifle scope, you only need to use some basic hand tools.

title: The Pistolcraft Founder: Are Lt. Col. Jeff Cooper’s Teachings Still Relevant?
description: Lt. Col. Jeff Cooper, a pioneer in pistolcraft and founder of the American Pistol Institute, is credited with developing these fundamental safety rules. In today’s article, veteran law enforcement instructor Mike Boyle questions if Cooper’s rules are as relevant today as they were when they were first introduced
author: The Gun People

description: In today’s article, Capt. Dale A. Dye, U.S.M.C. (ret.) describes his experiences in visiting that historic volcanic island.
